definition: Birth or coming-into-being, especially as conditioned by becoming in dependent origination.
notes: OSF editorial authority now treats Buddhadasa Bhikkhu as primary for this term. Birth remains the default because it still carries the strongest reusable force in the standard sequence, but OSF now explicitly treats the moment-to-moment birth of I and mine as the primary explanatory frame for this term in dependent-arising practice discussions.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose emphasizing process rather than event
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when the English line benefits from it.

context_rules.context: When the passage emphasizes the movement from becoming into aging-and-death
context_rules.notes: Keep the place of the link within the chain explicit.
Notes may explicitly unpack this as the birth of I and mine or the arising of suffering in immediate experience.

context_rules.context: In OSF practical dependent-arising explanation of the repeated arising of selfing and suffering
context_rules.notes: Default retained, but explicitly interpreted through the Buddhadasa line.
